question: Will the S&P 500 be between 6900 and 6924.9999 on Apr 10, 2026 at 4pm EDT?
reasoning: The S&P 500 closed at 6,782.81 on April 8, 2026, after a massive 2.51% rally following Trump's announcement of a two-week ceasefire with Iran. With futures trading around 6,822 and strong momentum from geopolitical tension relief, the index needs only a 1.7-2.1% gain to reach the 6900-6924.99 target range by April 10. Given the positive momentum from oil price drops, reduced geopolitical risk, and historical patterns following sentiment extremes, this probability appears significantly higher than the 4% market price suggests.
key_factors: Current S&P 500 level at 6,782.81 only 1.7% below target range
Major geopolitical relief rally from Iran ceasefire announcement
Oil prices dropped 16% reducing inflationary pressures
Historical data shows 16% average gains following extreme bearish sentiment readings
risks: Ceasefire could break down before April 10
Only 1.3 trading days remaining until resolution
Market could consolidate after large rally
Economic data releases could cause volatility
Target range is narrow (only 25 points wide)
